codeReview.summary: The linter JSON appendix validation IS correctly implemented and working (extractJsonAppendix() at line 186 parses ```json blocks, validateJsonAppendix() at line 240 validates against schema, JSON parse errors produce proper error messages). However, the commit cad2a1b associated with this feature does NOT contain any linter-related changes - it only contains .factory/validation/m1-foundations/user-testing/ files for spec/docs assertions. The feature verification in the transcript confirms the linter works (exit code 0 for valid files, exit code 1 for invalid JSON), but no corresponding code changes were committed.

issues.description: Commit cad2a1b files changed are only .factory/validation/m1-foundations/user-testing/flows/spec-docs-validation.json and synthesis.json - these are NOT related to linter-json-appendix-validation. The commit message states 'm1-foundations round 1 - all 10 spec/docs assertions pass' which is about SPEC.md/schema.json alignment, not JSON appendix validation.
issues.suggestion: If the linter JSON appendix validation was already implemented and just needed verification, the commit should contain a verification report or at minimum reference that no code changes were needed. The current commit attributes the work to a completely different feature.

issues.description: The linter code itself correctly implements JSON appendix validation - extractJsonAppendix() uses regex /```json\s*([\s\S]*?)\s*```/i to extract JSON, validateJsonAppendix() validates against schema, and produces proper json-parse errors for invalid JSON syntax.
issues.suggestion: No code changes needed - implementation is correct.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker was assigned 'linter-json-appendix-validation' but the commit created is for 'm1-foundations round 1 - spec/docs assertions'. These are distinct pieces of work - the former is about linter code, the latter is about documentation/spec alignment.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff commitId: 'cad2a1b' but commit shows only .factory/validation/m1-foundations/user-testing/ files modified. Transcript shows worker tested linter successfully but commit is for different work.

sharedStateObservations.observation: When a feature requires only verification (no code changes), the commit should clearly state this and reference what was verified. The current commit makes it appear the work was spec/docs validation when it was supposed to be linter verification.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff 'whatWasImplemented' states 'The linter...already correctly implements JSON appendix validation...No code changes required.' but commit message and files don't reflect this verification work.

summary: FAIL - The linter JSON appendix validation implementation is correct and the worker verified it works (all 3 sample files pass, invalid JSON produces parse errors). However, commit cad2a1b does not contain any linter-related changes - it only modified validation files for m1-foundations spec/docs assertions. The commit is misaligned with the feature it supposedly represents.
